episodes.title: Episode 03 — Waking Up
episodes.description:
In Episode 03, I get into the part of my story where things really started to crack. After the secret relationship I talked about last episode, I got married, eventually divorced, and went through my “wild phase” — and somewhere in all of that, the doubts I’d pushed aside my whole life finally caught up with me.
I talk about the exact moment it happened: sitting in a meeting, listening to the explanation about the 144,000 partakers, and realizing the story didn’t add up no matter which way you looked at it. That sent me down a rabbit hole — reading, researching, printing out their own publications — and eventually to a parking-lot conversation with my dad that ended with one word: “apostate.”
This is the episode where I stopped faking it. Still unscripted, still just me on the drive.
If you grew up in it or left later in life, I’d love to hear from you: was there one specific moment that woke you up, or was it slow? Drop it in the comments.

episodes.title: Episode 02 — My Double Life as a Jehovah’s Witness
episodes.description:
I lived a double life as a Jehovah’s Witness — pioneer and ministerial servant on the outside, someone completely different underneath. Episode 02 picks up where my story left off: being held up as an “exemplary youth” while privately feeling like a fraud, what happened after high school, and a secret that shaped years of my life.
This channel is stories, humor, and honest talk from the driver’s seat — where I came from, what I’m into, and whatever’s on my mind on the drive. This episode covers the exhausting reality of keeping up appearances, getting out of high school, pioneering, and the long road through reproval and reinstatement. Recorded on my drive home.
New episodes whenever I’m solo on a longer drive. If you grew up in something similar, I’d love to hear your story in the comments.

episodes.title: Episode 01 — Raised a Jehovah’s Witness
episodes.description:
Raised a third-generation Jehovah’s Witness — this is where my story starts. Episode 01: how my family got in, what childhood in a strict JW household was really like, and the stories that stuck with me.
I grew up a Jehovah’s Witness, left years later, and built my own path. This channel is stories, humor, and honest talk from the driver’s seat — where I came from, what I’m into, and whatever’s on my mind on the drive to work. This first episode is all origin: three generations of family history, meetings three times a week, the rules around holidays and outsiders, and a few stories from growing up (including the Ninja Turtle incident).
New episodes whenever I’m solo on a longer drive. If you grew up in something similar, I’d love to hear your story in the comments.
